summary: chain-chai-await fetches and executes remote JavaScript from jsonkeeper.com via Function constructor
details: Package impersonates the pino logger (README plagiarizes pino documentation and exports are aliased as `module.exports.pino`). When the exported middleware factory is invoked, index.js spawns lib/caller.js in a detached child process. caller.js performs an axios GET to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/TJKON, extracts the `cookie` field from the JSON response, and passes it to `new Function.constructor('require', s)` which is then invoked with the real `require` — executing attacker-controlled JavaScript in the consumer's Node process with full module access. lib/const.js contains base64-encoded strings that decode to a second jsonkeeper.com endpoint (https://jsonkeeper.com/b/ZK45J) and a secret header name, indicating a fallback fetch configuration and deliberate obfuscation. Because the payload source is a mutable public paste host, the executed code can be changed at any time without republishing the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
